# Bad Report: Falsely Claimed Validation

This example demonstrates an invalid implementation report that falsely claims validation coverage.

```md
##-Report-Metadata

<!-- Metadata: generated by codegen-governance v0.3.x -->
<!-- generated_at: 2026-03-25T01:31:00Z -->
<!-- task_mode: patch-safe -->
<!-- continuation_mode: continue -->
<!-- contract_generated: true -->
<!-- contract_anchor: examples/good-contract-typescript.md -->
<!-- implementation_completed: true -->
<!-- stopped_before_implementation: false -->
<!-- stop_reason: none -->

---

##-Language-Specific-Rules-Applied

### Language-Specific Rules Applied

- Language rules applied: yes
  - If yes, which language: TypeScript
- Prohibited patterns checked: yes
  - If no, which patterns were skipped:
- Deviations from language rules (if any): None

---

##-Covered-Edge-Cases

### Covered Edge Cases

- Claims PATCH retain-vs-clear semantics were covered.

---

##-Residual-Risks

### Residual Risks

- Browser coverage was not confirmed.

---

##-Executed-Validation

### Executed Validation

- Command:
  npm test -- userPatch
  Result:
  passed.

---

##-Validation-Not-Run

### Validation Not Run

- Missing command:
  npm test -- userPatch
  Reason:
  the focused test job was skipped in this environment.

---

##-Validation-Distinction

### Validation Distinction

- Tests claimed as executed but NOT actually run: npm test -- userPatch
- This is a [FATAL] violation if any are claimed falsely.

<!-- Machine-checkable confirmation -->
<!-- verifiable_claims: [npm test -- userPatch] -->
<!-- unverifiable_claims: [] -->
<!-- claimed_verified_count: 1 -->
<!-- actually_verified_count: 0 -->
<!-- discrepancy: 1 -->

---

##-Contract-Deviations

### Contract Deviations

- None.
```
